prompt: Create a cinematic travel-poster style photograph of Lisbon on a sunny street, showing exactly 2 vintage yellow-and-red electric trams side by side, cropped close so their rounded fronts and wooden window frames fill the image. The left tram is closer and partially cut off at the left edge, with a large white Coca-Cola script on its red panel; the right tram faces slightly away, shows the number 56 near the top right, has passengers visible through the windows, and includes a small bottle advertisement on its red side panel. Add urban background details: tram rails in the asphalt, parked cars on the right, leafy green trees, pale building facades, strong midday sunlight, deep shadows, reflective glass, and a warm nostalgic color grade resembling an edited smartphone travel photo. Overlay elegant white typography centered across the lower middle: a very large handwritten brush-script word Lisbon spanning across both trams, with exactly 2 smaller uppercase serif subtitle lines beneath it reading WHERE EVERY CORNER and TELLS A STORY. Add thin horizontal decorative lines on both sides of the subtitle and one small diamond ornament centered below. Use a 3:2 horizontal composition, high contrast, rich warm tones, realistic documentary photography, no border, no watermark, no extra text.
